title: Distimake cissoides
intro: Distimake cissoides, also known as Mexican buckeye or Mexican buckthorn, is a shrub or small tree native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, offering potential health benefits in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Distimake cissoides include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are often used topically to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.

It belongs to the genus Distimake, which is a genus of flowering plants native to the Americas. This plant is often found in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Distimake cissoides is also supported by molecular phylogenetic studies.
